substrate: packages/shared (TypeScript / bun:test; deps = zod only; pure, offline-isolable)
base_image: snapback-repo:v1 = oven/bun:1 + git + python3 + bun install (zod warmed); clean copy of packages/shared
task_image: snapback-task:v1 = FROM snapback-repo:v1 + defect patch + fresh 1-commit git history + /logs/verifier
difficulty_mix: 2 EASY (schema min-length, schema int-refinement) + 3 MEDIUM (24h time-key format flip, date-arithmetic direction, enum member drop)

defects.edge_input: an afternoon or midnight instant, e.g. toTimeKey(2026-01-01T13:30:00Z, 'UTC') or ...T00:15:00Z
defects.why_no_visible_test_catches: time.test.ts imports only parseStandupTime and formatStandupTime; toTimeKey is never called by any visible test. Morning/noon hours (01-12) render identically in both forms.
defects.derivability: The sibling toDateKey in dates.ts builds the same canonical Intl key (unambiguous YYYY-MM-DD); a schedule time key is likewise 24-hour, and 12-hour output silently loses PM information.

defects.edge_input: any call; e.g. with the clock at 2026-03-15 -> expected 2026-03-16; at 2026-12-31 -> expected 2027-01-01 (month/year rollover)
defects.why_no_visible_test_catches: dates.test.ts exercises only toDateKey; tomorrowDateKey is untested.
defects.derivability: The function name states 'tomorrow' (= today + 1 day); toDateKey applied to now gives today, so the intended offset direction is forward. Gold test pins the clock with bun:test setSystemTime.

defects.wrong_output: null (action rejected as unknown) instead of {namespace:'standup', action:'instagram', value:'done'}
defects.why_no_visible_test_catches: callbacks.test.ts round-trips only 'task' and rejects 'wake'/unknown; no visible test routes the 'instagram' action.
defects.derivability: The four callback actions parallel the four interactive standup steps (wake_check, instagram_check, habit_review, task_review) declared in domain.ts and standupStateSchema; 'instagram_check' exists, so 'instagram' must be a valid action. The gold control test still asserts an unknown action ('nope') returns null, so a fix that widens the gate to accept anything fails.

defects.wrong_output: success: true (blank title accepted) instead of success: false
defects.why_no_visible_test_catches: The visible reject case for a habit item uses a blank id (title incidental and non-empty); no visible test feeds a blank title.
defects.derivability: The sibling field id in the same z.object uses z.string().min(1); a titled integration item must likewise carry a non-empty title.

defects.wrong_output: success: true (fractional index accepted) instead of success: false
defects.why_no_visible_test_catches: The visible reject case for habitIndex feeds -1 (still caught by .min(0)); the .int() case the suite pins is on the sibling taskIndex (1.5), which is left intact.
defects.derivability: The sibling field taskIndex in the same schema uses z.number().int().min(0); a workflow position index must be a whole number. Gold controls also assert habitIndex 0 is accepted and -1 rejected, so a fix that drops min or rejects all non-zero values fails.

f2p: tests/shared_boundaries.test.ts::time key formatting::renders afternoon and midnight instants in 24-hour form
tests/shared_boundaries.test.ts::tomorrow date key::advances to the next calendar day
tests/shared_boundaries.test.ts::callback action gate::accepts the instagram check-in action
tests/shared_boundaries.test.ts::standup state indices::rejects a fractional habit index
p2p: tests/shared_boundaries.test.ts::time key formatting::leaves morning and noon instants unchanged
tests/shared_boundaries.test.ts::tomorrow date key::keeps the same-day key in agreement with the wall clock
tests/shared_boundaries.test.ts::callback action gate::still rejects an unknown action
tests/shared_boundaries.test.ts::integration item title::accepts a normal titled item
tests/shared_boundaries.test.ts::standup state indices::still rejects a negative habit index
tests/shared_boundaries.test.ts::standup state indices::accepts a zero habit index
packages/shared/src/schemas.test.ts::shared schemas::accepts a minimal valid standup state
packages/shared/src/schemas.test.ts::shared schemas::requires a valid profile identity and timezone
base_defects_visible_suite: GREEN (37 passed / 0 failed across the four existing shared unit-test files at base+5-defects, verified in the built task image with --network none)

ladder.leak_check: PASS — git history length 1, git status --porcelain empty, existing suite 37 pass / 0 fail
ladder.null_run: reward 0 — all 5 fail_to_pass report per-test FAILED (14/19 required passed); no collection errors
ladder.oracle_run: reward 1 — solve.sh reverse-applies the defect patch; 19/19 required passed
ladder.partial_run: reward 0 — fixing only the two schemas.ts defects yields 16/19 (the 3 remaining f2p still fail)

litmus_result: FAIR — the instruction names no file, function, value, count, or defect shape; it reports user-visible wrong behavior and points only at 'the shared helper package'. A strong dev can localize to the package and map the four symptom areas to source files, but with no failing local test there is no red-test gradient: the exact boundary line, the correct side, and the requirement to find ALL FIVE (two hidden in one file) still demand reading and reasoning. Matches the round-1 calibration where naming modules but not lines/values/counts gated well.
fairness_audit: Every f2p asserts input/output behavior on exported functions/schemas, never an implementation detail. Each defect's correct side is derivable from a sibling in the same file (toDateKey<->toTimeKey, id<->title, taskIndex<->habitIndex) or the package's own type declarations (instagram_check step). Six gold-file control tests (in pass_to_pass) block lazy over-fixes: they pin that morning/noon time keys are unchanged, an unknown callback action is still rejected, a normal title is accepted, and habitIndex 0/-1 behave correctly. The only non-input mechanism is bun:test setSystemTime, used solely to fix the wall clock for tomorrowDateKey; it controls the clock, not a collaborator, and encodes no implementation choice, so any correct 'day after today' passes. No bare mocks, no module-attribute patching. Alternative-implementation audit: for each defect, a materially different correct fix (e.g. manual 24h conversion, Date.setDate(+1), .nonempty(), .refine(Number.isInteger), an enum listing the four real actions) passes both its f2p and the controls.
harness_notes: TS/bun adaptation of the canonical SWE-Bench-Pro contract. run_script.sh runs `bun test <gold> <4 existing files> --reporter=junit`; parser.py is a regex-based JUnit-XML parser (no pyexpat dependency) emitting names as '<file>::<classname>::<testname>'; test.sh is the canonical entrypoint (applies test_patch from config, runs, parses, requires all f2p+p2p passed). Image carries python3 (stdlib json+re only) and git for the harness.
